def: s.f. Custom, habit, manner, wont, usage, practice

Malice prepense ; deliberate malice. adāvat-i-jiballī, aslī, yā zātī , Natural antipathy; animosity. adāvat-i-dilī, yā qalabī . Secret malice or enmity. adāvat rakhnā , v. n. To bear malice. adāvat se , adv. Maliciously; feloniously. adāvat nikālnā , v. a. To resent; avenge oneself.

more: adj. Malicious; felonious. adāvatī nālish . A malicious prosecution.

Half formed; incomplete. ad-bīch , adv. In the midst or middle; during completion. See bīch . ād-pāo , The one-eighth of a seer. ad-peṭ, ādhā peṭ adj. lit . Half a stomach full. Insufficient (food); deficient; not enough. Ad-peṭ khānā miltā hai. ad-ja'lā , adj. Half burnt; half consumed.
adj. Wonderful, astonishing, surprising; extraordinary; strange, curious, rare; admirable; — s.m. Surprise, astonishment, &c.
subs: s.m. An astonishing feat, a miracle
wety: From Sanskrit अद्भुत (adbhuta, “supernatural”).

def: adj. S. अति exceeding, भू to be. Pālī. adbhuto See. achraj . Hidden; mysterious; wonderful. Dekho re, koī jogyā hamār, adbud rūp banā-wane-hār? Mag. Song. Khemṭā of Shiv . Say, have you seen my jogī—master of a thousand shapes and graces?
s. or adv. (dakh.) Midst, in the midst: see بيچ bīch.
adj. (dakh.) See ادبهت adbhut.
n. A semicircle. ad-choṭ , E. adj. Slightly wounded or hurt ( chuṭīlā ). Sāṅp ad-choṭ nikal gagā .

ety: S. अट्ट+क; and cf. S. हट्ट, H. हाट
def: s.m. The shed or place in which kahārs, palanquin bearers, coolies, carters, &c. assemble or abide; stand, station, assembly-room; lounge or meeting-place of idlers or pleasure-seekers; — a square frame for embroidery; perch of a bird-cage; heel of a shoe; one of the (usually) two divisions of land watered by a well (see āḍ); — a fixed measure or standard
subs: s.m. The keeper or owner of an aḍḍā.
wety: Borrowed from Northwestern Indo-Aryan. Ultimately related to Prakrit *अड्डइ (aḍḍaï, “to obstruct, stop”), from Dravidian.

def: s. (dakh.) Joist ( Harris ).
n. m. 1. Half a yard, chain, etc. 2. A half measure; a pint. 1. Ek addhā pilwāo, to kām bane . 2. Botal chāhȳë, ki addhā?

def: n. f. 1. Half a damṛī ; one-eighth (W.), or one sixteenth (E.) of a pice. Addhī kī bhaṅg, terī mūchhoṅ pe raṅg! Bhale mānas kā laṛkā chamārī ke saṅg! Phakkaṛ (Doggerel). A farthing eighth of bhang , and what a curl in your moustache! The son, of gentle birth, and living with a cobbler's wife! 2. Half a piece of malmal (Indian muslin). addh'yal , Mag. adiyā'hī , n. f. A paper-kite worth one addhī (half a pie). Bolo, be lamḍo, "addhyal kī dhīrī hai!" Cry out boys, "the half pie has turned tail!"
s.f. Half a damṛī, or half a pāʼī; half-piece (thān) of cloth.
more: s. f. Half a damṛī (a small coin), half a piece of cloth.

def: adj. Worth one addhī, or half a pāʼī; s.m. Anything worth half a pāʼī.

s.m. Order, command, injunction; instruction; prediction; a form of salutation among jogīs and other religious mendicants to a superior, good day, good morrow; good-bye, farewell; (in Gram.) the substitution of one letter or syllable for another.

n. m. S. आ + दिश् to point out. 1. Command, order ( āgyā ); bidding, direction. 2. ( In Gram .) One letter substituted for another; an inflection. ādes , intj. 1. A form of salutation among jogīs, faqirs , etc. from an inferior to a superior. Yeh samjhā, banāvaṭ kā kuchh bhes hai. Lagā kahne, "Jogī jī, ādes hai ." Mīr Hasan. He saw through her disguise, and straightway he Saluted her—"Good morrow, Jogī jī ." 2. Farewell; good-bye; adieu. Bastar rāj ke taj dȳo, lȳo phakīrī bhes, Ab ham yāṅ se jāë haiṅ, lo sub ko hai ādes . Play. Chaubolā . His royal robes he threw aside, a beggar's garb put on, [Then turned and said], "Farewell to all, from hence this day I'm gone."
more: s. m. f. An order, a command; a salutation of Jogīs.

def: s.m. Procrastination, evasion, shuffling

adj. (f. -ī), Half; — s.m. A half-measure; a half-chain, half-yard, &c.
more: adj. S. अर्ध Pāli. addho, aḍdho . 1. Half. 1. Ādhī ko chhoṛe, sārī ko dhāë, Aisā būṛe, thā na pāë . Prov. Who leave the half to run after the whole Outrun themselves and miss the goal. [All covet, all lose]. 2. Izzat kī ādhī bhalī, be-īzzatī kī sārī kuchh nahīṅ . Prov. Better the half with honor than the whole with disgrace. 3. Ādhā tītar, ādhī baṭer . Prov. lit . Half partridge, half quail. [Applied to mixed speech or jargon]. 2. Tender; soft; quick of feeling; susceptible. Par-desī kā dil ādhā hoṭā hai . Prov. The stranger has but half a heart (He is very sensitive). [The natives inculcate tenderness towards the stranger, because, away from home and friends, his feelings are easily wounded.] 3. ( Bot .) Half inferior. ādhā bhāṛā,—kirāyā , n. f. Half hire. ādhā-sī'sī, ādā-sīsī , W. adh-kapālī , E. n. f. S. शिरस H. sīs head. Ped. dard-i-shaqīqā . Pain affecting the half of the head; faci…
adj. Half. In compos. it becomes آده ādh or اده adh or ادها adhā or اد ad; as, آده جلا ādh-jalā, Half-burnt; ادهموا adh-mūā or ادموا ad-mūā, Half-dead.

def: adj. S. अधम lowest. 1. Low; mean; humble (condition); inferior. 2. Vile; despicable; contemptible. adh'am , n. m. A great sinner, offender, etc. ( pāpī ). Koṭin patīt, mahā aprādhī, nām lȳe tir jāt adham . Mag. Bhajan . A million times a sinner great taking His name shall cross the Styx.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic أَدْهَم (ʔadham).

more: n. m. S. धा to conceive. Conception; pregnancy. ādhān se ho'nā, hāmlā ho'nā, pānī churānā (in jest), aṅḍyānā (birds), Wom. pāoṅ bhārī ho'nā, peṭ se ho'nā, ās se ho'nā, dheṅḍā phulānā (in contempt), v. n. To be pregnant; to be with child; to be in the family way. adhān'ik , n. m. A ceremony practised as conducive to conception.
s. m. 1. A ceremony practised as conducive to conception. 2. Conception, pregnancy.

n. m. S. दह् to heat. Hot water for cooking victuals. Aghan, chūlhe adhan . Prov. When Aghan (Nov-Dec.) comes, the pot boils on every hearth.
s.m. Oils, unguents. (Prop. in H. अद्हान ad-hān).
s.m. One of the thirty-six Rāginīs that are played or sung during the night only. (Also written aṛhānā and aṛānā).
E. n. m. One of the thirty-six Rāgnīs played or sung during the 2nd watch of the night only.
n. m. H. adh half, aṅg body. Palsy affecting one side or half the body; hemiplegia. adhaṅ'gī, adhraṅ'gī , adj. Palsied; paralytic.
more: s. m. Palsy affecting one side or half the body, hemiplegia.

s.m. A ceremony performed after cohabitation to procure or favour conception.
adj. & adv. Without prop, support or fastening; unsupported; dangling, pendulous, suspended; not fixed or firm, loose; separate, detached; (cf. next)
more: s. m. The lower lip, the lip.
s. m. Empty space, the space between heaven and earth.

def: s.m. The lower lip, the lip; the lower region or part; pudendum muliebre; the space between earth and sky, mid-air; empty space; middle; intervening space; interval; interim; — adj. Inferior, low, insignificant, vile; — adv. In mid-air, in the middle or midst, &c. (see adhar-meṅ); stiltedly; on tip-toe
subs: (adhara+amrita), s.m. Nectar of the lips; moisture of the lips
v.t. To raise or lift up in the air, to raise from the ground, raise on high
s.m.f. One whose mind is engrossed with sublunary matters; a person of inferior or superficial understanding
v.n. To walk stiffly, stiltedly, or affectedly; to walk on tiptoe
adv. In the middle, in the midst (of); midway; between; in the interval.

def: adj. S. अ not, धृ to support. 1. Unsupported; suspended; separate. Havā ke ūpar jo āsmān kā be-choba khemā ye tan rahā hai. Na uske mekheṅ, na haiṅ tanābeṅ, na uske chobeṅ, adhar khaṛā hai . Nazīr. Stretched out above the air, an unpropped canopy's the sky, No pegs, no ropes, no poles, it unsupported stands on high. 2. Loose; not fixed or firm. Terī aṅgūṭhī par nag adhar dharā, Kin ne jaṛī hai sachche hīroṅ kī . Song. adhar uṭhā le'nā , v. a. To lift up in the air; to raise from the ground. adhar chalnā , v. n. To walk on tiptoe; to mince; to walk affectedly or coquettishly. Zamīn pe adhar chalte haiṅ nāzse, Chalan ek jahāṅ se judā ho gayā . Anvar. With lovely grace, she skims the earth, Unique, apart from all the world. adhar rah'nā , v. n. To remain suspended in air; to hang in the middle. Adhar rahe woh nār; ādhī andar, ādhī bār . Riddle, Ans. khūṅṭī a peg. Half way she stops, half within and half without. adh'ar, adh'bar , n. m. 1. Empty space; the space between earth and sky. 2. Interval; interim ; intermission; interlude; the while. adhar meṅ, adham meṅ, ad bīch —, adv. In the midst of; half way; between; in the middle.
more: adv. H. i this, dhar place. S. इतः here. Here; hither; on this side; this way; on the spot. idhar se udh'ar ho'nā,—kī dun'yā udhar —, v. n. 1. To be lost, gone, made away with. Ghar kī chīzeṅ idhar se udhar ho gaīṅ . Wom. 2. To be displaced, disarranged, disordered. Bhalī chaṅgī rakhī rakhāī, idhar se udhar ho gaī . 3. To be inverted, subverted, turned upside down. Idhar kī dunyā udhar ho jāë, par bāt nahīṅ pherūṅgā . The earth may be turned upside down, but I will not go back from my word. idh'ar udh'ar, jid'har tid'har , Rus. īṅ' ge ūṅ'ge, ja'hāṅ ta'hāṅ , etc. adv. S. इतः ततः 1. Here, there, and every where; this way and that. Gayā hai jab se tu dil ko le-kar, nahīṅ hai mujh ko qarār ek jā, Ummīd milne kī tere rakh-kar, idhar udhar hūṅ maiṅ jātā ātā . Nazīr. Since you have gone and taken my heart, no where can I find rest, In hopes of meeting you I wander here and there unblest. 2. All…

Tir. om'har , Rus. and Brij. ut . E. aṭ'ṭhe, o'ne, un'ne, uṅ'har, o'hor, hum'ar , W. uṅg'he, ūṅ'ghe, ū'māiṅ , Jāt. kaṛe , Māṛ. uṭhine , adv. H. wā , & o that, dhar place. S. ततः there. S. उत् beyond, धा to place. There; thither; on that side; that way. udhar se , adv. From that, or the other side; that way.

def: s.m. Support, prop, stay; stand, pedestal, base (of a geom. fig., &c., syn. bhūmi and neʼo); supporter, patron; subsistence, nourishment, food, aliment; meal; — dike, canal; basin round the foot of a tree; comprehension; location.
more: n. m. S. आ all round, धृ to support. 1. Food; victuals; subsistence; provision. 1. Hāth kā hatyār, peṭ kā ādhār . Prov. A tool in the hand, food for the stomach. 2. Ek ghaṛī kī be-hayāī, sāre din kā adhār . Prov. A moment's shame, a day's food. (A sarcasm on the shameless). 2. Fodder; provender; pasturage; keep. 3. Enough; a bellyful; sufficiency. Amīr kā ugāl, garīb kā ādhār . Prov. The rich man's spew, the poor man's food. 4. Support; stay; staff; patron. 1. Mo ko tere nām kā ādhār, Tū hai sāṅchā Parvārdigār . Song Bhajan . My trust is in thy name, Thou art the true Lord God. 2. Kāhū ke dhan, māl haiṅ; kāhū ke parwār: Tulsī Dās garīb ke Rām nām ādhār . Tulsī Dās. Some folk have wealth, and some relations own, Poor Tulsī Dās's stay is Rám alone. 5. The base, S. आधार, , A. قاعِدَه H. neo . ādhār ho'nā , v. n. To be enough; to suffice. Ādh ser āṭe meṅ ādhār ho jāëgā .
s. m. Food, aliment, victuals, nourishment, support.
s. m. A patron, supporter, one on whom dependance is placed for assistance.

(in compos.) An eater; as, دوده آدهاري dūdh-ādhārī, One who lives on milk. ماس آدهاري mās-ādhārī, Flesh-eater, carnivorous.

def: adj. Pālī. adhammiko . 1. Unrighteous; impious; irreligious. 2. Wicked; sinful; immoral; iniquitous. 3. Wrong; unfair; unjust; inequitable. 4. Unkind; hard-hearted; unfeeling; pitiless; merciless; without compassion. 1. Maëke meṅ rahtī, khusī man rahtī, Sāsre meṅ adharmī kar gayo, rī, Dagā chhail gajbī kar gayo, rī . Rus. Song. Wom. In my maternal home had I remained, I had been happy; but this cruel man Hath thrown me in his father's, mother's house, This gay gallant hath ruthless taken me in. 2. Baisākh meṅ mere ḍhig ābat hai, Mo ko naṅgo sej pe ḍārat hai, Nāhīṅ soban det, nā sobe adharmī, "E sakhī, sājan?" "Nā, sakhī, garmī ." Wom. Riddle. Mukrī . In May he comes up close to me, And throws me naked on my bed. Nor lets me sleep, nor sleeps himself, Most cruel tyrant that he is. "A lover, dear?" No dear, "the heat." 5. Cruel; fell; unrelenting; inhuman. Adharmī ko jīu mārte darad nā āë . Rus.

Pop, adj. The half of anything, generally half a maund. Ser ko dūdh, adhaun ko pānī, Ghammar ghammar phire mathānī . Prov. Ghī meṅ ādhoṅ ād kī chhāch hai . 2. A kind of barter, half the weight of another article being given in exchange. Nā, Bir, nā, ādham ūd, nā; lāb kāṛ-ke de de . Rus. Wom. No sister, no; not half. You should give me only a handful less (than the weight of mine). ād'hoṅ ādh kar'nā, adhyā'nā , v. a. To halve; to divide into two parts; cut in two, or halves; bisect; to go halves.

adj. Additional, extra; augmented; exceeding, surpassing, excelling; excessive; abundant; superior, greater; very great; more numerous; much; more; most; — adv. & postpos. Exceedingly; abundantly; excessively; greatly; very; in excess (of); too much; too great; over and above; in addition (to); besides
more: adj. S. अधि over, Pālī. adhiko . 1. Excessive; exceeding; overcharged; exorbitant; ultra ; superabundant; abundant; exuberant; redundant ( bahut ). 2. Extra; additional; augmentative; augmented. 3. Major; greater; more; most. 4. Superior; higher ( baṛā ). 5. Extravagant; profuse; lavish; prodigal. 6. Excellent; excelling; exquisite; surpassing; superlative ( umda ). adh'ik , adv. 1. Over and above; overmuch; too much; very Dekhan meṅ woh gāṅṭh gaṭhīlā, Chākhan meṅ woll adhik rasīlā, Mukh chūmūṅ, to ras kā bhāṅḍā, "Kyoṅ sakhī, sājan?" "Nā, sakhī, gāṅḍā ." Wom. Riddle. Mukrī . To sight, all knotted o'er is he, To taste, a juicy morsel sweet. Suck him with kisses sweet, and you Shall draw a cup of liquor sweet. "A lover?" "No, a sugarcane." 2. Without measure; without stint. Phr. Over head and ears; enough and to spare; satis superque. adh'ik ho'nā , v. n. To be in excess; to be over.
adj. Exceeding, excessive, greater, more, most, additional, augmented.
subs: (-se), To be more (than); to be in excess (of); to exceed, be over, &c.

To nominate; appoint; commission. 3. To authorize; accredit; delegate; commit to the hands of; warrant; licence; to leave it to one. 4. To install; invest; induct; elect. adhkār rakh'nā , v. n. 1. part. act. (of عدو Heb. To pass) 1. Passing. 2. Superseding. 3. Transgressing. 4. adj. Unjust, wicked. 5. (substant.) An enemy. 6. adj. (irreg. from عادت but much used in Hindūstān) Accustomed, addicted. عادي کرنا āﻌdī karnā, To addict.
subs: (-kā), To be accustomed (to), to be addicted (to).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عادی ('ādī), from Arabic عَادِيّ (ʕādiyy).

def: part. adj. & s.m. Passing; passing beyond; transition; superseding; exceeding (a limit); transgressing, acting wrongfully or unjustly, &c.; unjust, wicked; — an unjust man; a transgressor; an enemy.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عادی ('ādī), from Arabic عَادِيّ (ʕādiyy).

def: s.m. A teacher of manners (adab); teacher; — adj. Polite, courteous.
more: n. m. Ped. A teacher of etiquette or manners.
s. m. A teacher of polite manners, urbanity, &c.
wety: From Arabic أَدِيب (ʔadīb).

ety: بدي badī, — rt. S. वद् 'to speak,' — redupl.
def: s.f. Predestination; fate, destiny; — allegation; slander, calumny.
more: n. f. S. आदि first वद् to speak. 1. Fate; destiny; predestination. Is kī adī badī usī ke hāth se thī . 2. Slander; backbiting; calumny. Kisī kī adī badī se kyā hāsil? adī badī , adj. 1. Settled; fixed; appointed. 2. Predetermined; preordained; preconcerted. ad badā-ke , adv. Voluntarily; inevitably; nolens volens . Phr. By all means; by all manner of means.

more: (from آد आदि q. v.) adj. Relating to the first; and the rest, et cetera.

ety: inf. n. of عدل 'to act equitably,' &c.
def: s.m. Equity, justice, rectitude
more: n. m. Justice (انصاف). adl k. , v. a. 1. To do justice; to decide according to equity and good conscience. 2. To judge; decide. adl-gustarī , n. f. The administration of justice.
s. m. Justice, equity, rectitude. عدل کرنا aﻌdl karnā, To do justice. عدل گستري aﻌdl-gustar-ī, s. f. The dispensing, distribution, or administration of justice.
subs: v.n. To do justice, to act equitably, to decide according to equity and rectitude; to judge (between), to decide
s.f. The dispensing, or the administration, of justice.

def: s. m. Exchange, alteration. ادلا بدلا کرنا adlā badlā karnā, or ادلي بدلي کرنا adlī badlī karnā, To exchange, to swop or barter, to confuse, to displace.
v.t. To exchange, barter; to change; convert; trasmute; transfer; shift; displace; confuse; transform; pervert.
v.t. To exchange, barter; to change; convert; trasmute; transfer; shift; displace; confuse; transform; pervert.

adj. Gluttonous.

s.m. A descendant of Adam; a human being; man; individual, person; adult; a sensible, or honest man; mankind; people; husband; wife; servant, attendant, retainer
more: s. m. f. A descendant of Adam, a human being (man or woman), an individual, people.
subs: v.t. To humanize, civilize; to make a rational creature of; to teach manners
adv. Per man; severally, individually; one by one
or ho-jānā, To become a man, attain to manhood; to become civilized, learn manners, &c.; to recover one's reason; come to one's senses.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian آدَمِی (ādamī), from Arabic آدَمِيّ (ʔādamiyy, “human”).

def: n. m. 1. A descendant of Adam; a human being. Baṅgālī jo ādmī, to paret kaho kis ko? Prov. If a Bangālī is a man, what is a devil? 2. An individual; person; man. 3. A grown-up person; an adult. Bachchā to kyoṅ, khāsā ādmī thā . 4. An intelligent person; a wise man; a skilful man. Tum apan ko ādmī jānat ho! E. Rus. Prov. You think yourself a man! 5. An honest man. Ādmȳoṅ kā kāl hai . Honest men are scarce. 6. Mankind; the human race; nation; people; souls; inhabitants; the public; population; community; folk. Ādmī ne kachchā shīr pȳā hai . Prov. A creature reared on new milk is man. [The Natives have a saying that man is kachcha (crude and liable to err) because he has drank his mother's (new) milk]. 7. Husband; lord; consort; wife. 8. A servant; retainer; follower; attendant. Are ādmī! Here, fellow! ādmī banānā,—karnā , v. a. To humanize; educate; civilize; teach manners; polish. Karne lage phir wohī samajh kī bāteṅ, Bāre, hameṅ ādmī banāyā tū ne . Momin. ādmī pīchhe , adv. 1. Each person; per head; apiece. 2. Severally; each; one by one; individually. ādmī honā , v. n. 1. To grow up to manhood; to come of age. Ab tum ādmī hue, hosh pakṛo . 2. To learn manners, politeness, etc. Ākhir yūṅ hī hote hote ādmī ho jāëgā . 3. To become a man again; recover from insanity or intoxication; to come to one's senses (after making a beast of oneself). kā'lā ādmī , n. m. 1. A black man; a negro. 2. A native of India; an Indian (used by Europeans.
s. f. See آدم گري.
s.f. Human nature, humanity; benevolence, compassion, sympathy; civility, urbanity, politeness; good breeding; rationality, reason, judgment
subs: To be bereft of (one's) humanity; to lose (one's) reason, be out of one's senses; to lose one's manners
To become humanized or civilized; to acquire good manners or politeness; to recover one's reason, come to one's senses
ādmīyat-meṅ lānā, v.t. To humanize or civilize; to teach manners; to bring to reason, or to one's senses
To practise humanity; to act as a man.

def: n. m.— muī , n. f. S. अर्धमृत. 1. Half dead; in the agonies of death. Sainan kī bāt kachhū samjhī nāhīṅ maiṅ ne, Mo ko ad-marā kar-ke dhar gayo rī. Dekho! mo pe gajab chhailā kar gayo rī . Wom. Rus. Song. I knew not what those glances meant, Half dead he's left me quite, What ruin, see, that gay gallant has wrought! 2. Starved; weak; helpless.

n. f. 1. Human nature; humanity. 2. Fellow-feeling; sympathy; tenderness. Kuch hotī ādmȳat, gar hote ādmī, Ye khūb-rū to hūr hue, yā parī hue . Zauq. Had they been human, then humanity they'd have, These beauties of the race of houris, fairies are. 3. Civility; urbanity; good manners; politeness; good breeding; courtesy; amenity; gentleness; affability; etiquette. Ādmȳat aur shaë hai, ilm hai kuchh aur chīz, Kitnā tote ko parhāyā, par woh haivāṅ hī rahā . Zauq. Humanity is one thing; learning, quite another. Teach a parrot ever so much, a parrot he will be. 4. Judgment; reason; discrimination. ādmȳat uṭh jānā , v. n. 1. To be divested of one's human nature. 2. To lose, or forget one's manners. Āj kal ke laṛkoṅ kī ādmȳat uṭh gaī . 3. To lose one's right reason; to be out of one's senses. ādmyat pakaṛ'nā,—sīkh'nā , v. n. 1. To become civilized, humanized. 2. To acquire gentility, good manners, or politeness. Ādmȳat sīkho, be ḍhaṅge mat phiro . 3. To come to one's senses,; to be restored to one's reason. Ādmȳat pakṛo, bahko mat . ādmȳat sikhānā,—meṅ lānā , v. a. 1. To civilize; teach manners ( ādmī banānā ). 2. To bring one to (his) senses.
more: s. f. Humanity, civility. آدمیت پکڙنا ādmīyat pakaṛnā, To become civilized. آدمیت سکهلانا ādmīyat sikhlānā, or آدمیت مین لانا ādmīyat meṅ lānā, To civilize. آدمیت کرنا ādmīyat karnā, To behave with humanity.

Who slaudereth the innocent shall have neither funeral rites nor salvation. 2. Irreproachable; unimpeachable; pure. Phr. Innocent as the babe unborn; with clean hands; with a clear conscience.
